sutta: 4 8 monks these 5 are good man's gifts squad 5 he gives a gift in faith with deference in time with unconstrained heart he gives a gift without hurt to self or others and in giving a gift in faith wheresoever the full result of that gift ripens there comes wealth riches and great property and he is fair to look upon handsome but the wondrous beauty of the lotus and in giving a gift with deference wheresoever the full result of that gift ripens his children and wife his slaves messengers and workforce hearken diligently unto him lend ear and serve him with understanding heart and in giving a gift in time wheresoever that ripens there comes wealth and the goods that come to him in time are abundant and in giving a gift with unconstrained heart wherever that ripens there comes wealth and he bends the mind to enjoy the fullness of the fivefold joy of the senses and in giving a gift without her to sell for others wheresoever the full result of that gift ripens there comes wealth riches and great property and never from anywhere comes heard to his property either from fire or water rajas or thieves or empire's as verily monks these are the 5 gifts of a good man that's the end of the suta
commentary: now this is quite a good suita uh it tells you the result of giving with different conditions that a person who gives um with faith has been having a lot of faith because in giving you always the result of giving is always that wealth comes back to you riches and great property and of course depending on how much you give and also depends on how virtuous you are and the person to whom you give but when a person gives in faith having a lot of faith in addition to welfare he is born very beautiful or very handsome and the second 1 when the person gives with respect then the result of it in addition to wealth then the children and wife slaves messengers and work workers all listen to him and serve him with an understanding heart that's the result of showing respect so because you show respect when you give her then in addition to wealth and those people around you all show respect to you the third 1 the person gives in good time in addition to welfare he he the wealth comes to him also in a good time when he needs it whenever he needs it and um giving in good time means when a person really needs it at that time and that is the time to give like when somebody asks you for help and that person is really desperate and you pretend uh you have something else to do or you have nothing to give him and another day when that person is rich and then you go and maybe make him a gift but that's not the time he wants i mean he's already rich you make a gift to him he doesn't appreciate it's only when he is very poor and is in need you help him that's the type of person i usually hear when we think of our friends when we think of those now we have done as a service and you all we always remember the person who helped us now when we were down and out when we really needed help uh that is the the type of person who is a true friend uh so when a person gives in good time like this he will also the the wealth will come to him in good time also then the another 1 the fourth 1 giving with unconstrained heart that means that some people give it a constrained heart you know in other words for example they they want to give then they think i yeah i'm gonna give 10 dollars is too much i think i'll give 5 dollars 5 dollars too much now i think anybody make a gift of 2 dollars or 1 dollar so or sometimes he make a gift after giving then he goes home then he regrets so if you regret that then your blessing is reduced yeah so the result of it now is next time when you be in your next life or something you you're born and there's a lot of wealth you you come by a lot of wealth you won't know how to enjoy the joy cannot arise because at the moment when you gave that gift you had no joy in you so next time when the result ripens and great wealth comes to you you have no joy in it you don't know how to enjoy it it's a well-known story in the dhammapada commentaries about a man who made a gift to apache buddha he was a very stingy person in that story and his wife liked to give to monks and renuncians who came by the house and beggars and all that but he never approved of his wife giving to these people who came to back for food he thought he's wasting his wealth he thought he should save all that food all that expenditure and instead give it to his workers because his workers will work for him in return so 1 day he was in a very good mood and he told his wife that the wife could give whatever food she would she'd like to give that day so happened the pacheco buddha came by and the wife cooked some good food and then gave it to that particular buddha and when the bachelor buddha left the house and walked away he happened this this stingy man happened to come back and he saw this particular buddha this this monk going away and having come from his house he knew that this monk must have got some food from his wife so he stopped the monk and he asked the monk to open his or he he opened the monk's bowl himself to see what was in the bowl and when he saw the good food in the bowl he closed the lid and then he went back and i was not happy i thought his wife wasted all this good food so because of that in the the the next 7 times because of having given to a particular buddha the next 7 times he was born as the richest man in that town in that town for 7 lifetimes he was the richest man but every time he was born as a rich man he was so stingy he used all the old things and he ate also stale food you know never knew how to enjoy his wealth he was as good as a poor man for 7 lifetimes at the end of 7 lifetimes having used up his good fortune in heaven not having planted uh um new blessings after that lifetime he went to hell because his good karma bank account was depleted every time we come and enjoy life man we are using up our good karma bank account our blessings bank account if you don't know how to replenish it it all gets used up so you must know you understand the law of karma you know as we enjoy now we keep saving doing good deeds and so that our blessings bank account now married bank account will increase and we can continue to enjoy value and then the last 1 giving a gift without hurt to the to self or others and this is very important that means when you give a gift it must not harm other beings you must not harm yourself not harm other beings means you must not go for example go to the market and you select a chicken a live chicken or a fish and ask them to slaughter and then after that you go and make an offering that is causing harm to others causing harm to yourself means that um you make that offering and it harms you in some way and from this suta actually you find a contradiction with our jataka tales because in our jataka tales there's a story about how the bodhisattva or bodhisattva in his previous life uh through himself to feed down the hill committed suicide to fill to feed a tiger the hungry tiger and the cubs and this contradicts the suta that's why that jatakathir is not quite acceptable because it's not in the original discourses of the buddha into something that was compiled later as a kind of folk tale and also the other jataka story about how the buddha gave away his wife and children to a beggar that is also not acceptable because when you give away at least if he had given away his wife and his children to a millionaire a wealthy man so that they have a good life that is understandable that to give the wife and children away to a beggar who beat the children and probably beat the wife and that is it's not logical so there are a lot of these kinds of contradictions if we when we study the suta we have to think uh reflect on it then you will see some of these points the next few times
